draft.title: a CRM for freelancers
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a CRM for freelancers

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: A CRM for freelancers benefits from intimate, approachable interface elements that feel supportive rather than corporate
facade.label: Warm sidebar
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward human-centered, inviting design with soft colors, rounded forms, and a feeling of personal guidance
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer clean, minimal, or cooler-toned interfaces that feel more professional or neutral; less emphasis on warmth and emotional connection

evidence.hypothesis: A CRM for freelancers benefits from intimate, approachable interface elements that feel supportive rather than corporate
evidence.implication: User gravitates toward human-centered, inviting design with soft colors, rounded forms, and a feeling of personal guidance

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to personality-driven copy that feels approachable, paired with professional-grade features
facade.axisTargeted: Voice and personality: playful/friendly vs. professional/serious
facade.label: Playful tone, serious tools
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers personality and warmth in the interface—copy like 'Let's crush those invoices' or friendly onboarding; branded voice over neutral
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers clean, professional, no-nonsense language—minimal copy, neutral tone, business-standard terminology; trust built through competence, not personality

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers need to visualize project deadlines and milestones as a visual timeline rather than a dense list or table
facade.label: Client timeline view
facade.acceptImplies: User values visual chronological storytelling over tabular data; prefers seeing 'when things happen' at a glance; likely wants to spot scheduling conflicts and project overlap visually
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers structured, scannable data tables; wants precise dates/metrics over visual patterns; may find timelines cluttered or less actionable than sortable columns

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer instant, pixel-perfect positioning of contact cards and task blocks over dragging with magnetic springs
facade.axisTargeted: snappy instant positioning vs fluid animated snapping
facade.label: Snap to grid
facade.acceptImplies: The CRM should use discrete snap-to-grid interactions for organizing contacts, projects, and deadlines—no easing or momentum. Direct. Satisfying. Done.
facade.rejectImplies: Freelancers want smoother, physics-based animations when moving or organizing tasks—slight momentum, gentle easing, surfaces that feel weightless and responsive rather than mechanical.

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers need persistent client access + scannable project overview, so a sidebar nav + card-based main area reduces cognitive load vs tabs or lists
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick access to clients/projects without tab-switching; prefers visual chunking over dense rows; likely wants persistent context
facade.rejectImplies: User finds sidebars cramped or cluttered; prefers tabs or flat structure; wants maximum screen real estate for details; may need faster search-based workflows vs browsing

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer earthy, approachable color palettes over stark contrasts, signaling trust and calm in a tool for managing client work
facade.label: Warm neutral tones
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward softer, human-centered interfaces; may favor beige, tan, soft gray, warm white backgrounds over cold blues or pure black
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers cooler, more minimal, or higher-contrast aesthetics; may value clarity and edge over warmth; suggests clinical or modern-tech positioning

axes.label: Whether they prefer minimal, clean interfaces vs. rich visual feedback and data density for client/project overview
Whether they want centralized dashboard control vs. distributed, modular tool panels for different freelance workflows
Whether they respond to professional/formal tone vs. casual, encouraging tone that motivates solo work
Whether they want calm, distraction-free focus space vs. energizing, motivational environment for productivity
Whether they prefer flat, simple data views vs. layered, contextual information that shows relationships between clients/projects/invoices
Whether they want instant, snappy interactions vs. smooth, guided transitions that help them understand workflow steps
axes.poleA: Quick and snappy or smooth guidance

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer minimal, clean interfaces vs. rich visual feedback and data density for client/project overview
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they want centralized dashboard control vs. distributed, modular tool panels for different freelance workflows
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they respond to professional/formal tone vs. casual, encouraging tone that motivates solo work
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they want calm, distraction-free focus space vs. energizing, motivational environment for productivity
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer flat, simple data views vs. layered, contextual information that shows relationships between clients/projects/invoices
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they want instant, snappy interactions vs. smooth, guided transitions that help them understand workflow steps
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: A CRM for freelancers balances the need for comprehensive information display with cognitive ease; grid-based organization provides structure without feeling chaotic or overwhelming
facade.label: Calm grid layout
facade.acceptImplies: User values visual order and breathing room; prefers modular, scannable interfaces that reduce decision fatigue when managing multiple client relationships
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer either denser information density with visual hierarchy or a more flowing, less grid-constrained layout; seeks either efficiency through compaction or fluidity through organic arrangement

draft.title: Freelance CRM Dashboard
draft.summary: A clean, mobile-first CRM interface for freelancers to track clients, projects, and invoices. Designed with warm, professional colors and intuitive navigation.
